summary: The available literature indicates that grandmultiparity is not an independent risk factor; instead, its link to poor perinatal outcomes stems primarily from reflecting a history of previous adverse pregnancy events—such as preterm birth or fetal growth restriction—which are themselves well-established predictors of later complications. Because grandmultiparity co-varies strongly with existing risk factors, it lacks standalone prognostic power when adjusted for those established contributors.
